segments.text: [Speaker Notes] Chapter 21, Opener, Packaging eukaryotic DNA within the nucleus. In order to fit within the nucleus, DNA is wrapped about histone core particles, as described in this chapter, forming a fiber some 11 nm in diameter. This fiber is further condensed by winding into a greatly thickened and shortened structure, which undergoes additional winding and compaction until it can fit within the nucleus.
